unexpected_finds.title: pdflatex build cleanly resolved all 11 \cite keys in one bibtex pass without missing-citation warnings
unexpected_finds.detail: All 11 cited bibkeys (bt_baseline_note_v10, papanokechi_pcf1_v13, papanokechi_pcf2_v13, lane2_dee3c01, lane2_six_item_verdict, lane2_independent_substrate_verification, lane2_independent_depth_probe, phase_a_deg_a_zero_064, cf_value_audit_pcf2_9impls_065, pcf1_v13_v_quad_row_reframing_066, costin2008asymptotics_followup) resolved on first bibtex pass; .blg log shows zero warning$ entries. Build pipeline (pdflatex → bibtex → pdflatex ×2) produced final 5pp PDF in clean state on first attempt; expected at least one undefined-reference iteration.

unexpected_finds.title: Page count converged to 5 pp (upper end of 3-5 pp target band) with the four-row table rendered on its own column-width spread
unexpected_finds.detail: First pdflatex pass produced 4pp; after bibtex + bibliography rendering the count rose to 5pp. The 12-row table at §2 (\Cref{tab:fourrow}) renders comfortably and consumes about 1/2 of a page including caption. Section length distribution: §1 ~1/2 pp, §2 ~1 pp + table, §3 ~1/2 pp, §4 ~1/3 pp, §5 ~1 pp, §6 ~1/3 pp, Acks + AI Disclosure + Bibliography ~1 pp.

judgment_calls.title: Cite v1.0 §4.2 open-content prose at the inclusive range L481-487 rather than the narrower L484-487 cited by the relay prompt
judgment_calls.reason: On-disk reading of bt_baseline_note.tex shows the sentence beginning 'The V_quad upper branch is consistent with the borderline-locus mechanism' starts at L481, not L484. Citing at L481-487 captures the surrounding lead-in 'The d=2 upper branch ... not recovered by ...' that frames the open-content sentence, and is a strict superset of the prompt's L484-487. SHA-anchored citation eliminates ambiguity.

judgment_calls.title: Use project shorthand 'PCF-1 v1.3 §6 Theorem 5' rather than canonical '§5 Theorem 5.1'
judgment_calls.reason: Inherited from R1 wording (lane2_meta_verdict.md L42), 064 §5 (phase_a_supplementary_deg_a_zero.md L222), and 066 §2 (which also uses '§6 Theorem 5'). Both labels resolve to the same SHA-anchored prose at p12_pcf1_main.tex L566-577. The PCF-1 v1.4 §6 row-table amendment (forward-pointed in 067 §6) will canonicalise the project shorthand at the source-prose level under the PCF1-V13-RECONCILE jurisdiction.

judgment_calls.title: Deposit 067 in bridge folder sessions/2026-05-07/ (chronological successor to 066) rather than 2026-05-06
judgment_calls.reason: Bridge HEAD 9261c79 (066) commits 'Wed May 6 21:17:57 2026 +0900' but the bridge tree-path is sessions/2026-05-07/ for 066. Operator routing convention places W20 mid-week post-LANE-2 cascade artefacts at sessions/2026-05-07/. 067 keeps that pattern; LANE-2 + 064 + 065 remain at sessions/2026-05-06/.

judgment_calls.title: Use suffixed bib key costin2008asymptotics_followup to avoid future collision risk with v1.0 bib
judgment_calls.reason: v1.0 annotated_bibliography.bib defines @book{costin2008asymptotics,...}. If a future omnibus build (e.g., a combined v1.0 + follow-up Zenodo bundle) ever co-loads both .bib files, an unsuffixed key would be silently overwritten by the latter. Using the _followup suffix is a defensive collision-avoidance strategy and does not affect single-document compilation. Other shared keys (papanokechi_pcf1_v13, papanokechi_pcf2_v13) intentionally retained for cross-document citation parity.

judgment_calls.title: Avoid forbidden verbs entirely (not just in prediction context) for STEP 4 conservative-PASS
judgment_calls.reason: Relay STEP 4 specifies HALT on prediction-context hits. Conservatively zero-hit total (case-sensitive on the regex \b(shows|confirms|proves|establishes|must)\b) eliminates any ambiguity over what counts as 'prediction context'. Substituted with: records, states, observes, captures, asserts, identifies, reads, takes, carries, aligns. Same scan discipline as 066 STEP 5 PASS-at-0-hits.
